findings.control_description: S3 bucket ACLs must not be writable by AllUsers or AuthenticatedUsers. WRITE_ACP permission enables attackers to modify the ACL itself, granting themselves FULL_CONTROL and escalating to read, write, and delete all objects.

evidence.why_now: Asset has been unsafe for 240 hours (threshold: 168 hours). Unsafe since 2026-01-01T00:00:00Z.

remediation.description: Bucket grants ACL modification (WRITE_ACP) to public or authenticated users. An attacker can call PutBucketAcl to grant themselves FULL_CONTROL, then read or modify all objects.
remediation.action: Remove WRITE_ACP grants from the bucket ACL and remove policy statements granting s3:PutBucketAcl or s3:PutObjectAcl to public principals. Enable S3 Public Access Block with BlockPublicAcls set to true.
